ending: Gerald Torith is executed after his records expose the domestic conspiracy, although his supposed foreign connection is never established. King Edwin intends to abdicate when Edgar returns, with Edward succeeding him and Edgar taking command of the armies. That transition has not happened when Indria and Asteris invade Cydaria through forbidden portals. Alana begins evacuations, while Osian's brother Riven commands the attackers. Inside the raid dungeon, the expedition has cleared an uncommon survival trial and a legendary dragonkin trial, but more sequential trials remain under the 168-day limit. Avery and Edgar leave the legendary trial with Lyra and Blitz, Jasper contracts a darkness drake, and Tyron bonds Rocky while arranging to forge Derek a void-attuned glaive. Savannah's household continues its business and crafting work through The Void Emporium. At level 200, Derek chooses a Void path beyond his local system's supported limits and becomes the mythical Void Monarch. He refuses to sever his bond with Silvi, causing both of them to enter linked evolutions whose duration and outcome remain unknown.
in_short: After Gerald Torith's capture, Derek helps stabilize his Savannah household, profits from the Crown auction, and accepts Edgar's invitation to a level-250 raid. Gerald's information exposes a domestic conspiracy, and King Edwin executes him, but Indria and Asteris invade Cydaria while much of its elite force is trapped in the dungeon. The raid uses Derek's Time Prison as a secure, accelerated base, survives an uncommon beast-tide trial, and clears a legendary dragonkin battle. Avery bonds Lyra, Edgar bonds Blitz, and Tyron prepares to forge Derek a weapon from wyvern materials. Meanwhile, Brandi advances mana-core weapons and Malorie, Rudy, Roman, and Natalie establish The Void Emporium's new business. At level 200, Derek rejects safer class paths and chooses Void, becoming the mythical Void Monarch. His transformation also pulls Silvi into an evolution in Savannah, leaving both companions' condition unresolved as the invasion and remaining dungeon trials continue.

recaps.text: Previously: Derek Hunt established a household in Savannah with Silvi and allies including Brandi Fields, Malorie, Jacks, Rayna West, and Rudy. His rare void affinity and Time Prison made him an unusually powerful adventurer with growing ties to Cydaria's Crown. Gerald Torith had become a direct danger to Derek's circle and to the royal family. The conflict culminated in an attack at the palace, where Derek, Edgar, and Avery overcame Gerald's group and secured the surviving attackers. Jacks also carried an unresolved grievance against Torith connected to the kidnapping and murder surrounding his family.

So far this book: Derek transfers Gerald and three allies from Time Prison into Natalie's secure custody. Natalie and Alana begin tracing Gerald's secrets, while Jacks accepts Gerald's imprisonment as the best chance to protect his son and learn the truth about his family. Derek then turns to the Crown auction, where void-beast meals and Roman's permanent-stat potions command extraordinary prices. King Edwin restores Walter's family from the disgraced Gracefall name to Searidge and removes the soul contract that had suppressed Walter for decades. The auction ends with Edwin buying a powerful vitality elixir and Natalie purchasing the most valuable meals. Derek also accepts the Walking Forge's offer of a temporary halberd and a custom glaive to be made when the smith's other commitments allow.

So far this book: Edgar invites Derek into a level-250 raid, hoping Time Prison can serve as a secure camp with altered time. Derek distributes royal skill scrolls and permanent-stat resources among his household, releases its members from their obligations to him, and accepts the expedition with their support. Silvi chooses to remain in Savannah and protect the others. Alana and Natalie separately promise oversight during Derek's absence. Roman creates a potion that permanently raises mental attributes, and Malorie is assigned to negotiate its distribution. Derek reaches the capital with raid supplies, where Edgar adds Walter as a healer and reveals that Vanessa, formerly connected to Gerald's operation, is now constrained by a strict kingdom oath and contract. The full group of twenty-five reaches the unstable dungeon and prepares to enter.

So far this book: The dungeon gives the expedition 168 days to clear seven sequential trials. Vanessa leads ten members into the first, an uncommon ten-day beast-tide challenge, while Derek converts Time Prison into a comfortable shared base for those waiting outside. The smith begins designing Derek's custom glaive. Elsewhere, King Osian and his brother Riven plan to invade Cydaria with a second country while its elite fighters are absent. In Savannah, Natalie obtains exclusive rights to Roman's mental-stat potion through The Void Emporium, with Malorie and Rudy completing the agreement. Derek spars with Edgar and confirms that his defenses can withstand the prince's lightning. To reduce the subjective wait for the inactive raiders, most of the group enters Time Prison under altered time while Derek and the smith remain outside. Brandi, meanwhile, produces a working water-core sword prototype.

So far this book: Brandi's improved water- and wind-core weapons reduce the mana cost of matching elemental skills, and Thomas leaves for the capital Academy with equipment and guidance from Jake and Jensen. Gerald's records expose most of Cydaria's domestic conspirators, but his claim of an Indrian connection remains unsupported. King Edwin executes Gerald for murder, trafficking, and treason, plans for Edward to succeed him, and intends Edgar to lead the armies. At the same time, Indria and Asteris begin an invasion through forbidden portals, prompting Alana to organize evacuations. In the dungeon, Vanessa's entire trial party survives the ten-day beast tide despite severe injuries that Tristan begins repairing. The next trial is legendary. Edgar, Derek, Avery, Jasper, and Tara enter a ruined city and face three enormous wyverns. Derek and Edgar kill the wind champion, then Derek and Avery defeat the death-attuned wyvern, though Derek's halberd breaks during the fight.

So far this book: Avery contracts the psychic amphitheer Lyra, and Edgar forces the surviving fire wyvern to accept a contract before naming him Blitz. Their bonds transform both creatures into legendary companions, while Avery also rises to a legendary class. The trial continues with another dragonkin wave. Lyra uses mental control to help Derek capture several possible companions inside Time Prison, and Jasper contracts a darkness drake. After the group kills the remaining wyverns, the combat phase ends. Each participant receives a dragon-related skill: Avery gains independent winged flight, Edgar gains defensive dragon scales, Tara gains Dragon Skin, and Jasper gains stronger regeneration. The party returns to the expedition with its new companions. Tyron agrees to turn the death-wyvern materials into a void-attuned glaive and armor for Derek, with Derek's direct help required during the forging.

So far this book: Tyron contracts and bonds a dragonkin worm named Rocky, whose evolution makes him a legendary companion. Derek reaches level 200 and prepares for his final class change by improving his void abilities and specializing in polearms and bare-fist combat. The system offers powerful time and space classes, but its Void option lies beyond its own supported limits. Derek chooses Void and receives help from another system, which recognizes his Void affinity as legendary. He becomes the mythical Void Monarch and begins an evolution driven by Void energy. When advised to cut his bond with Silvi, Derek refuses. The preserved bond draws Silvi into a simultaneous evolution while she is in Savannah. The book ends with both transformations unfinished. The raid still has sequential trials ahead under its 168-day deadline, and Cydaria remains under active invasion while Edgar and many of the kingdom's strongest fighters are inside the dungeon.
